summary: The reproduction confirms that CoreDNS processes an unlimited number of DNS queries on a single TCP connection, overriding the miekg/dns library's safe default of 128 queries per connection. A single TCP connection was used to send 500 queries, all of which received responses, proving the MaxTCPQueries=-1 setting enables unbounded resource consumption.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should demonstrate that a single TCP connection to CoreDNS can process at least 500 queries without being terminated by a per-connection query limit. The miekg/dns library's safe default limit of 128 queries should be exceeded, confirming the MaxTCPQueries=-1 vulnerability.
actual_observed_result: Running the exploit locally against CoreDNS 1.14.3 built from the target repository: 500 out of 500 queries were successfully processed and responded to on a single TCP connection. The connection remained open and functional throughout the entire test, confirming that no per-connection query limit is enforced.
verification_method: The exploit opens a single TCP connection to the DNS server on port 5353, sends 500 sequential DNS A queries for unique random subdomains (test0000.example.com through test0499.example.com), and counts the number of valid DNS responses received. If the response count exceeds 128 (the miekg/dns safe default), the vulnerability is confirmed. This distinguishes the vulnerability from a normal DNS query limit where the connection would be closed after 128 queries.
evidence_produced_by_run: 500 out of 500 DNS queries processed on a single TCP connection without the server closing the connection
All 500 queries received valid DNS responses, confirming the full plugin chain was exercised for each query
The miekg/dns library's safe default of 128 queries per connection was exceeded by nearly 4x
CoreDNS version confirmed: CoreDNS-1.14.3, darwin/arm64, go1.25.0
limitations_or_blockers: Docker Desktop image persistence issues prevented full Docker-based reproduction; vulnerability was verified through direct local execution of the same exploit logic

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, builds CoreDNS for linux/arm64 in a golang container, then builds the reproduction image with the binary and runs the exploit.
